scenario: вңҲпёҸ Airport English вҖ” Checking Bags: 'I need to check a bag'
text:
рҹҺ§ YouGlish
At the airline counter, tell the agent: {{c1::"I have one carry-on and I need to check a bag."}}
explanation:
Native speed: 'aЙӘ hГҰv wКҢn kГҰВ·riВ·Й‘n Йҷn aЙӘ niЛҗВ·dЙҷ tКғЙӣВ·kЙҷ bГҰg'
Rules applied:
вҖў need to вҶ’ /niЛҗВ·dЙҷ/ (Flap T cross-boundary: /t/ of 'to' is flapped between /d/ and /u/ вҶ’ reduced to schwa)
вҖў check a вҶ’ /tКғЙӣВ·kЙҷ/ (linking C+V: final /k/ of 'check' links to 'a')

рҹ§  'Check a bag' means it goes in the cargo hold. 'Carry-on' is the small bag you bring on the plane.
usage:
• "I need to check two bags and I'll keep this backpack as my personal item."
  • "How much does it cost to check a bag?"
spanish: 'Tengo un equipaje de mano y necesito registrar una maleta.' вҖ” Registrando equipaje. FonГ©ticamente, 'need to' se reduce a 'nee-duh' con un Flap T, y 'check a' se convierte en 'che-kuh'.

scenario: вңҲпёҸ Airport English вҖ” Asking for Gate: 'Where is gate...'
text:
рҹҺ§ YouGlish
To find your departure gate, ask: {{c1::"Excuse me, do you know where gate B12 is?"}}
explanation:
Native speed: 'ЙӘkВ·skjuЛҗz mi, dК’Йҷ noКҠ wЙӣr geЙӘt...'
вҖў do you вҶ’ /dК’Йҷ/ or /jЙҷ/ (Yod Coalescence + schwa reduction)
вҖў gate B12 вҶ’ /ЛҲgeЙӘtМҡ biЛҗВ·twЙӣlv/ (unreleased stop: the /t/ of 'gate' is held and not released before the consonant 'B')

рҹ§  'Gate' is the specific boarding area. Note the unexploded /t/ at the end of 'gate'.
usage:
• "Is this gate B12 for the flight to Chicago?"
  • "My boarding pass says gate C5, is that past security?"
spanish: 'Disculpe, Вҝsabe dГіnde queda la puerta B12?' вҖ” Preguntar por la puerta de embarque. FonГ©tica: 'do you' colapsa en 'djuh/juh' y la /t/ en 'gate' queda retenida (sin explosiГіn de aire) antes del sonido de la B.

scenario: вңҲпёҸ Airport English вҖ” Flight Crew Requests: 'Can I get a blanket?'
text:
рҹҺ§ YouGlish
During a flight, to ask the flight attendant for something, say: {{c1::"Can I get a blanket and some water, please?"}}
explanation:
Native speed: 'kЙҷВ·naЙӘВ·gЙӣВ·rЙҷ blГҰЕӢВ·kЙӘВ·ЙҫЙҷn sКҢm wЙ‘В·ЙҫЙҷr pliЛҗz'
вҖў Can I get a вҶ’ /kЙҷВ·naЙӘВ·gЙӣВ·rЙҷ/ (schwa on 'can' + Flap T on 'get a')
вҖў blanket and вҶ’ /blГҰЕӢВ·kЙӘВ·ЙҫЙҷn/ (Flap T on 'blanket' before 'and' + H-drop/D-deletion on 'and')

рҹ§  Politeness rules: 'Can I get...' with a 'please' at the end is the standard friendly request pattern for in-flight service.
usage:
• "Can I get a set of headphones, please?"
  • "Can I get an extra pillow?"
spanish: 'ВҝMe podrГӯa dar una manta y agua, por favor?' вҖ” PeticiГіn a bordo. FonГ©ticamente, 'Can I get a' se pronuncia 'kЙҷ-naЙӘ-gЙӣ-rЙҷ' y el final de 'blanket' puede enlazarse como un Flap T suave antes de 'and'.
